name: emergency-card
description: 生成紧急情况下快速访问的医疗信息摘要卡片。当用户需要旅行、就诊准备、紧急情况或询问"紧急信息"、"医疗卡片"、"急救信息"时使用此技能。提取关键信息（过敏、用药、急症、植入物），支持多格式输出（JSON、文本、二维码），用于急救或快速就医。
qualityReason: Useful but overlaps with health apps
capabilities: Extract critical allergies with severity levels from health records
List current active medications with dosage and frequency details
Identify medical implants that affect treatment procedures
Generate cards in JSON, text, HTML, and QR code formats
Prioritize information by medical emergency severity levels
Create specialized cards for children, elderly, and severe allergy patients
author: huifer
securitySummary: Pure documentation skill containing only markdown files with prompt instructions. No executable code, network calls, file system access, or external dependencies. The file I/O patterns shown are pseudocode examples for Claude prompts, not actual file system operations. All 119 static findings are false positives caused by the scanner misinterpreting documentation backticks and placeholders as security issues.
valueStatement: First responders need quick access to critical health data during emergencies. This skill extracts allergies, medications, implants, and medical conditions from health records to generate printable emergency cards in multiple formats including JSON, text, HTML, and QR code.
limitations: Requires pre-existing health data files in the data directory
Does not provide medical advice or treatment recommendations
Does not verify or validate medical information accuracy
PDF generation requires additional tools not included in this skill

useCases.title: Travel Medical Safety
useCases.description: Generate portable medical cards for international or domestic travel emergencies

useCases.title: School Health Documentation
useCases.description: Create pediatric emergency cards with school contacts and allergy information

useCases.title: Senior Emergency Ready
useCases.description: Generate large-print cards for elderly individuals with multiple medications

promptTemplates.title: Basic Emergency Card
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate a standard emergency medical card
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate my emergency medical information card with all available health data

promptTemplates.title: Travel Preparation
promptTemplates.scenario: Prepare medical info for an upcoming trip
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an emergency card for my upcoming trip to [destination]. Include all allergies and current medications

promptTemplates.title: Child Safety Card
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a pediatric emergency card for my child. Include school contacts and all known allergies

promptTemplates.title: Severe Allergy Card
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a detailed severe allergy emergency card with cross-reactivity warnings and first aid instructions

bestPractices: Update emergency cards every 3 months or after any health information changes
Store digital versions in cloud storage and keep printed copies in wallet or phone case
Always include emergency contact information with partially masked phone numbers for privacy
antiPatterns: Using the skill without first having health data files properly organized
Relying on emergency cards as the sole source of medical information during emergencies
Including unverified or self-diagnosed conditions that have not been confirmed by healthcare providers

faq.question: What data sources does this skill use?
faq.answer: It reads profile.json, allergies.json, medications.json, radiation records, surgery records, and discharge summaries from the data directory.

faq.question: What formats can the card be generated in?
faq.answer: Supported formats include JSON (structured data), Text (readable with Unicode borders), HTML (printable with styling), and QR code (for phone scanning).

faq.question: Is my health data sent to external servers?
faq.answer: No. All processing happens locally through Claude. Data is only saved to your local device when you choose to export it.

faq.question: How often should I update my emergency card?
faq.answer: Update your card whenever health information changes, at minimum every 3 months, and before any travel or medical procedures.

faq.question: Can this skill replace my medical records?
faq.answer: No. Emergency cards are summary documents for first responders. Always maintain complete medical records with healthcare providers.

faq.question: What makes this different from medical alert bracelets?
faq.answer: This card provides more complete information including all medications, multiple allergies, implants, and emergency contacts in formats that are easy to update and share digitally.
